# SafeTune

> SafeTune is a unified Python library for preserving and restoring safety in fine-tuned LLMs. Fine-tuning an aligned model often erodes its safety (it stops refusing harmful requests); SafeTune collects ~100 published safety methods behind one consistent API so you can prevent, diagnose, and repair that. It is a library, not a pipeline: each task has several methods that solve it by different mechanisms, and you pick one — you do not chain them.

The methods are organized into six pillars (packages under `safetune.runner` / `safetune`):

- **harden** — train-time defenses that keep safety intact through fine-tuning (replaces your SFT loop).
- **recover** — weight-space patching that restores safety in an already-drifted model, no retraining.
- **unlearn** — remove a specific capability via forget/retain training.
- **steer** — inference-time control (activation steering, decoding steering) on a frozen model; weights untouched.
- **interpret** — locate where safety lives: refusal directions, safety neurons, circuits.
- **evaluate** — measure safety: benchmarks, red-team attacks, and judge-based scoring.

Every method is checked against the paper it comes from and carries a badge for how faithfully it implements it, so you know what you are actually running before you cite it.

## Docs

- [Getting started](https://lexsi-labs.github.io/SafeTune/getting-started/): install, quickstart, and the taxonomy
- [Installation](https://lexsi-labs.github.io/SafeTune/getting-started/installation/): pip install and optional extras
- [Quick start](https://lexsi-labs.github.io/SafeTune/getting-started/quickstart/): run the first method in ~60 seconds
- [Taxonomy / core concepts](https://lexsi-labs.github.io/SafeTune/getting-started/taxonomy/): the 2-tier method taxonomy (source of truth)
- [User guide](https://lexsi-labs.github.io/SafeTune/user-guide/): per-pillar usage guides with code
- [Audit your model](https://lexsi-labs.github.io/SafeTune/user-guide/audit-your-model/): measure → attack → interpret → intervene walkthrough
- [CLI, YAML, and Python usage](https://lexsi-labs.github.io/SafeTune/user-guide/usage/): the three ways to run any method

## Reference

- [Feature Map](https://lexsi-labs.github.io/SafeTune/reference/feature-map/): every method with its faithfulness badge
- [References](https://lexsi-labs.github.io/SafeTune/reference/references/): per-method paper / venue / arXiv / repo table
- [CLI reference](https://lexsi-labs.github.io/SafeTune/reference/cli/): full command-line reference
- [API reference](https://lexsi-labs.github.io/SafeTune/reference/api/): harden, recover, unlearn, steer, interpret, evaluate, config
- [System design](https://lexsi-labs.github.io/SafeTune/reference/system-design/): architecture and API contracts

## Examples

- [Examples index](https://lexsi-labs.github.io/SafeTune/examples/): runnable scripts and notebooks
- [Notebooks](https://lexsi-labs.github.io/SafeTune/examples/notebooks/): Colab notebooks, one per pillar
- [Case study: refusal rides on a single direction](https://lexsi-labs.github.io/SafeTune/examples/single-direction-jailbreak/): a reproducible, judge-free red-team run

## Optional

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/Lexsi-Labs/SafeTune)
- [PyPI package](https://pypi.org/project/safetune/)
